text.title: behavioral-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for behavioral-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Attaching observers without an unsubscribe mechanism in long-lived objects.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Memory leak: the subject holds a strong reference to the observer, preventing garbage collection. Always return an unsubscribe function or use WeakSet/WeakRef.

failure-mode.description: Applying a behavioral pattern when a simple function or data structure suffices.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A Strategy with one implementation, an Observer with one subscriber, or a Chain with one handler adds abstraction cost with no extensibility gain. Apply patterns off measured code smells (cyclomatic complexity, duplication, change frequency), not aesthetics.

failure-mode.description: Using class-based GoF patterns in languages with first-class functions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In Python, TypeScript, and Rust, many behavioral patterns reduce to higher-order functions or type system features. A Strategy pattern implemented as a callable function is idiomatic and requires no class hierarchy.

failure-mode.description: Over-engineering Observer chains without error handling or backpressure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unbounded Observer chains where a slow subscriber blocks notification or an exception propagates silently cause hard-to-debug cascading failures. Use reactive streams (RxJS, RxPy) when backpressure and error propagation matter.

failure-mode.description: Pattern-zealotry: applying Strategy with one implementation "for future flexibility".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forces a 2nd-impl-or-no-pattern rule. One-impl interfaces add ceremony without extensibility benefit.

failure-mode.description: Observer patterns generated by LLMs that forget to unsubscribe.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Memory leaks in long-lived processes. Require an explicit `dispose`/`close` in the template.

failure-mode.description: Visitor implementations that mismatch the entity hierarchy after edits.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Missed new entity types cause silent failures. Demand exhaustiveness checks (TS `never`, Python `assert_never`).

failure-mode.description: Command pattern code lacking idempotency keys when used for retried operations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duplicate retries execute the same operation twice. Require an idempotency key in the prompt for any networked command.
